# jjezs_paddock ŌéŻBB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ŌéŻA_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks ŌĆö kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test ŌĆö all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine ŌĆö a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health ŌåÆ marshal-zero ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract ŌĆö load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director ŌĆö all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch ŌĆö they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails ŌåÆ fixture stops ŌåÆ operator stops ŌåÆ debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape ŌĆö partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy ŌĆö is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test ŌĆö cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ŌéŻA_

Runs concurrently with ŌéŻA_'s remaining paces; ŌéŻA_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse ŌĆö locked design decisions (ŌéóBBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ŌéóBBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1ŌĆō26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected ŌĆö it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today ŌĆö bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 ŌĆö family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor ŌĆö lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ŌéŻA_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen ŌĆö surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` ŌĆö current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` ŌĆö `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` ŌĆö upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAC

## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy ŌĆö the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** ŌĆö `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** ŌĆö `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** ŌĆö `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** ŌĆö `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Muster + verify** ŌĆö `rbw-arm` (retrievers) and `rbw-adm` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** ŌĆö `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** ŌĆö phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** ŌĆö phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arm` and `rbw-adm` muster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAE

## Character

Third executable phase. The largest cloud-side phase by surface area: exercises every producer mode (conjure, bind, graft) plus reliquary inscribe and full consumer flow (vouch, summon, plumb, tally). This is the phase where the recent first-build spook bug class (kludge-aware-charge-prereq, ZRBOB_PROJECT, reliquary integrity) gets caught at construction rather than discovery.

## Work

Add Phase 3 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Inscribe reliquary** ŌĆö `rbw-dI` DirectorInscribesReliquary; verify reliquary stamp lands under `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}reliquaries/<date>/<tool>` with all canonical tools present (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo)
2. **Conjure small vessel** ŌĆö `rbw-fO` against a small conjure-mode vessel (suggest `rbev-busybox` or sentry-deb-tether); verify hallmark publication with shape `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/{image,vouch,pouch,about,attest,diags}`
3. **Bind small vessel** ŌĆö `rbw-fO` against a bind-mode vessel; verify digest pin and hallmark
4. **Graft small vessel** ŌĆö `rbw-fk` LocalKludge then `rbw-fO` graft path; verify GRAFTED vouch verdict and `<hallmark>/` shape (no pouch for graft)
5. **Vouch all three** ŌĆö `rbw-fV` mode-aware vouch; verify each verdict shape matches mode contract (SLSA for conjure, digest-pin for bind, GRAFTED for graft)
6. **Summon each** ŌĆö `rbw-fs` RetrieverSummonsHallmark; verify local image present
7. **Plumb compact + full** ŌĆö `rbw-fpc` and `rbw-fpf`; verify metadata fetch from new GAR layout
8. **Tally** ŌĆö `rbw-ft` RetrieverTalliesHallmarks; verify all three hallmarks classified correctly by health state
9. **Rekon** ŌĆö `rbw-irh` and `rbw-irr`; verify ark basenames match canonical members under hallmark and reliquary subtrees

Implicitly exercises the kludge-aware-charge-prereq fix path: k-prefixed kludge hallmarks classify correctly without false missing-vouch warnings.

## Verification

- All three hallmarks present in GAR with correct shape per mode
- Each vouch verdict matches mode contract
- Summon + plumb succeed against new layout
- Tally counts and classifications correct
- Rekon surfaces all expected canonical members
- No false missing-vouch warnings on k-prefixed paths

## Out of scope

- Crucible charge / security tests (Phase 4)
- Hallmark abjure (Phase 5)
- New vessel creation (uses existing kit-shipped vessels)

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAF

## Character

Fourth executable phase. Brings up containers; runs the security and capability fixtures against freshly-built hallmarks. This is where moriah's airgap test cases finally run on a depot whose first-time creation we just validated end-to-end. The user noted: "I've been pretending like I don't need to test depot creation but maybe I do" ŌĆö this phase is where that decision becomes operational.

## Work

Add Phase 4 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Charge tadmor** ŌĆö `rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`; verify sentry+pentacle+bottle up cleanly under prefixed names
2. **Run tadmor security suite** ŌĆö `rbtd-r.Run.tadmor.sh` (or single-case mode against the just-charged crucible); verify 34 security cases pass against a freshly-conjured tadmor hallmark
3. **Quench tadmor** ŌĆö `rbw-cQ.Quench.tadmor.sh`; verify cleanup
4. **Charge moriah** ŌĆö airgap nameplate; verify charge with airgap topology against fresh enshrined-base + airgap-conjured vessel
5. **Run moriah airgap suite** ŌĆö capability fixtures verify airgap discipline holds end-to-end against fresh-cloud-built hallmark
6. **Quench moriah**
7. **Charge srjcl + run srjcl-jupyter (3 cases) + quench**
8. **Charge pluml + run pluml-diagram (5 cases) + quench**

Sequential only ŌĆö fixtures share regime state and container/network namespaces. Per-fixture quench-after-test discipline so a failure in one fixture doesn't poison the next.

The moriah test specifically validates the airgap path that requires `rbw-dE` enshrine + airgap-mode `rbw-fO` to have completed in Phase 3 against the freshly-created depot. If Phase 3 was wrong, moriah fails here.

## Verification

- All four crucibles charge successfully against fresh hallmarks
- Tadmor security suite green
- Moriah airgap suite green (the test that motivated this whole heat)
- srjcl-jupyter green (3 cases)
- pluml-diagram green (5 cases)
- Charge/quench cycles leave no residual state between fixtures

## Out of scope

- Cleanup of hallmarks/SAs/depot (Phase 5)
- Test infrastructure changes (any test failures here surface in subsequent paces or follow-up heats)
- Adding new fixtures (this phase exercises the existing four)

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAG

## Character

Cleanup phase plus the actual first run. The tier doesn't ship until it's been run end-to-end against fresh state and surprises captured. Failures during the first run are *informative*, not blockers ŌĆö they document the surprises this whole heat exists to surface.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so divest colophons are available and unmake-absorbs-governor-cleanup is in effect.

## Work

### Cleanup phase implementation

Add Phase 5 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Abjure all hallmarks** ŌĆö `rbw-fA` for each ordained hallmark; verify clean removal via `rbw-irh`.
2. **Divest operational SAs** ŌĆö `rbw-arD` for retriever, `rbw-adD` for director (BBAAN cult-verbs). Per BBAAN: divest reads installed RBRA, performs REST DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file. Verify clean removal via `rbw-arm` and `rbw-adm` muster (empty observation facts expected).
3. **Unmake depot** ŌĆö `rbw-dU` PayorUnmakesDepot. Per BBAAN: governor SA cleanup folds into unmake (404-tolerant on absent governor). No standalone forfeit-governor step. Verify quota recovery.
4. **Final tally** ŌĆö `rbw-dl` shows no depots, `rbw-irh`/`rbw-irr` surfaces clean GAR namespace, `rbw-arm`/`rbw-adm` muster empty.

### First end-to-end run

Execute the FIRST end-to-end run of `rbw-tP` against actual fresh state:

1. Marshal zero, commit (using the new signature from Phase 0)
2. Install RBRAs from secure backup
3. Run `rbw-tP` ŌĆö full sequence; expect ~1 hour wall-clock
4. Capture every surprise ŌĆö failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX ŌĆö in a notch dedicated to this pace's findings
5. Fix small surprises inline within this pace (one-line bash fixes, message clarity)
6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAH

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAK

## Character

Theurge consumer pace. Rebuilds pristine-lifecycle fixture atop the new SA-management surface produced by the cult-verb foundation pace (BBAAN). Depends on BBAAN landing first. Character is mechanical ŌĆö the new shape is settled in the paddock; this pace adapts theurge to it. ## Scope

Pristine-lifecycle section restructures. BBAAJ's ┬¦2 (pristine-lifecycle-simple) and BBAAK's original ┬¦3 (pristine-lifecycle-credentialed) collapse into a single unified section containing three fused cases that share one throwaway depot for the run:

- Depot stand-up ŌĆö depot levy and the rbrr field install needed to satisfy downstream preconditions.
- SA cycle ŌĆö governor mantle, retriever invest, director invest, access-probes, divests of operational SAs.
- Depot tear-down ŌĆö unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup.

Test consumes per-row observation facts via type-extension lookup. All stdout-substring assertions retire from pristine-lifecycle code. Test-side primitives reduce to tabtarget invocation, file copy (assay ŌåÆ role production path), `Path::exists()` on facts, and one fact-read for depot project_id.

Manifest registration: add new cult-verb colophons; remove the colophons retired by BBAAN.

Cost reduction: one throwaway depot per fixture run, down from four under the original BBAAJ+BBAAK design.

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). Any subsequent fixture step that needs those credentials must re-invest first. The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle ŌåÆ invest ŌåÆ access-probes ŌåÆ divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case ŌĆö `Depot tear-down ŌĆö unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers ŌĆö including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports ŌĆö must point to the RBCC home. Pristine fixture is currently service-tier-broken pending BBAAM Step 5; this remap is part of the bring-back-online work for this pace.

## Constraints

- Depends on BBAAN landing first.
- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on first failure cleanly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
- Reuse paddock-locked patterns; do not re-engineer them here.
- Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.

- Phase 1+ of rbw-tP (BBAAC and onward).

# jjezs_pace ŌéóBBAAN

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (ŌéóBBAAQ spec, ŌéóBBAAR handbook, ŌéóBBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays ŌĆö singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (ŌéóBBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` ŌĆö six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ŌéóBBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`, `rbgg_muster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent and the `rbfl_muster_*` image-muster precedent.

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Muster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast ŌĆö DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **ŌéóBBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for muster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **ŌéóBBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **ŌéóBBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Muster):`); runs after this pace.
- **ŌéóBBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (ŌéóBBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (ŌéóBBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (ŌéóBBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).

content.text:
Sweep complete. Here's the picture:

## Three "muster" tenants competing for the word

| Tenant | Status | Site count | Cost to rename |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Job Jockey** `jjx_list` | Landed, owned by JJ | JJ-internal (jjrmu_muster.rs, JJSCMU spec, jjk-claude-context, etc.) | N/A ŌĆö JJ's native verb |
| **RB Image Muster** | Landed in code | ~30 sites across 6 files | High |
| **RB SA Cult-Verb Muster** (BBAAN) | Spec + dockets only ŌĆö **no code landed** | 8 sites | Low |

## Recipe Bottle Image Muster ŌĆö landed sites

**Code (8 sites):**
- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` ŌĆö three function defs (`rbfl_muster_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`) + `buc_doc_brief` strings + `buc_success "Muster complete..."` strings (~16 lines)

**Tabtargets (3 files on disk):**
- `tt/rbw-imh.DirectorMustersHallmarks.sh`
- `tt/rbw-imr.DirectorMustersReliquaries.sh`
- `tt/rbw-ime.DirectorMustersEnshrinements.sh`

**Spec/docs:**

- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc:124, 1195-1204` ŌĆö `:rbtgo_image_muster:` quoin + section

## Recipe Bottle SA Cult-Verb Muster ŌĆö proposed sites only

**Spec (RBS0-SpecTop.adoc):**
- L119, L132 ŌĆö quoin attributes `:rbtgo_director_muster:`, `:rbtgo_retriever_muster:`
- L1041-1042 ŌĆö cult-verb triad prose (invest/divest/muster + lowercase-m semantic-load justification)
- L1070-1081 ŌĆö director muster section
- L1108-1119 ŌĆö retriever muster section

**Pace dockets** (`.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`): heavy references throughout BBAAN, BBAAC, BBAAG, BBAAK, BBAAR, BBAAQ ŌĆö colophon scheme `rbw-arm`/`rbw-adm`, `RBYC_MUSTER`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`/`_directors`, observation-fact convention.

**No bash code, no Rust code, no tabtarget files exist yet.**

## Strategic decision

You have three rename strategies ŌĆö they differ in cost and outcome:

**(a) Rename only the SA cult-verb** (cheap; ~8 sites, all in spec/dockets, no code touch). Image muster stays. Consequence: Recipe Bottle still has "muster" as Image Muster, so when you say "muster" the listener still has to disambiguate RB-image-muster vs JJ-list-heats. Conflict reduced from 3-way to 2-way but not eliminated.

**(b) Rename only Image Muster** (expensive; ~30 sites including code, Rust constants, tabtarget filenames, spec filename). Doesn't fix the immediate problem ŌĆö BBAAN's new muster still conflicts with JJ.

**(c) Rename both Recipe Bottle uses to one verb** ŌĆö most thorough; eliminates RBŌåöJJ conflict entirely. Could pick **one new verb** that fits both "list image catalog" and "list service accounts" (both are read-only listing operations) ŌĆö natural since you've already noted the lowercase-`m` "no-cloud-change observation" semantic in the cult-verb design.

**(d) Rename both Recipe Bottle uses to two different verbs** ŌĆö preserves the per-domain distinction (image listing vs SA listing), more sites to update than (c).

My read: **(c)** is the cleanest if a single verb captures both senses honestly. The shared semantic is "enumerate registry/cloud objects of one type, no health detail, no mutation" ŌĆö words like `roster`, `tally` (taken ŌĆö health-aware), `enumerate`, `inventory`, `manifest`, `survey`, `census`, `catalog`, `roll`, `scry` (taken for crucible), `recite` come to mind. If you want to preserve the noun-as-verb ceremony pattern (mantle/charter/knight/forfeit), `roster` and `roll` fit best.

Which strategy do you want? And do you have a candidate verb in mind, or want me to propose a shortlist?

message.content: D is the right choice.  How about 'roster' for the service accounts and... hmm remind me of the current image verbs that exist?

content.text:
Image-domain verbs currently in play:

| Verb | Role |
|---|---|
| **rekon** | List members within a single hallmark/reliquary subtree (depth-1; `rbw-irh/irr`) |
| **muster** | List top-level identifiers across the catalog (`rbw-imh/imr/ime`) ŌĆö the one to rename |
| **wrest** | Pull image from registry (`rbw-iwh/iwr/iwe`) |
| **Jettison** | Delete from registry (`rbw-iJh/iJr/iJe`) |

Adjacent foundry-domain verbs (related but separate prefix `rbw-f*`): tally (health-aware list), vouch, summon, plumb, abjure, ordain, kludge.

The semantic gap "muster" fills: **flat enumeration of top-level catalog identifiers, no health detail, director-tier**. RBSIM L20 explicitly contrasts it with tally ("muster is a flat-list operation; tally is health-aware").

Candidates that don't collide with existing verbs above and preserve the noun-as-verb ceremony cadence:

- **roll** ŌĆö "Director rolls hallmarks" (very short, calls back to roll-call/muster-roll origin)
- **roster** ŌĆö but you've already claimed this for SA; reusing here defeats option (d)
- **canvass** ŌĆö "Director canvasses hallmarks" (means: survey systematically, no judgment)
- **inventory** ŌĆö accurate but flat-toned, less ceremonial
- **manifest** ŌĆö collides with `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` (Rust manifest module ŌĆö different meaning, would muddle)
- **catalog** ŌĆö accurate, plain
- **recite** ŌĆö "Director recites hallmarks" (calls back to enumeration ceremony)
- **enumerate** ŌĆö accurate, dry
- **survey** ŌĆö "Director surveys hallmarks"
- **census** ŌĆö "Director censuses hallmarks" (awkward as verb)

My pick from this list: **roll** (shortest, ceremonial ŌĆö the natural sibling to "muster" in military-roll-call vocabulary, which is exactly the role being filled). **canvass** is the runner-up if you want more syllables for ceremony weight.

Which verb? And shall I confirm the SA-side scheme too ŌĆö `rbw-arR`/`rbw-adR` for roster (capital R since the symmetric `rbw-aD` divest is also capital, and lowercase-m precedent was specifically for "no cloud change observation" ŌĆö which roster also satisfies, so could be `rbw-arr`/`rbw-adr` lowercase to keep the case-as-semantic-load rule)?

message.content:
Keep same colophons, and don't change the cases when you don't know why.  Lowercase 'r' for non mutating listings.

For image, word needs to not start with r or w or j, because of the conflict with those verbs.  List more 5 letter options for this that do not conflict.

content.text:
Five-letter candidates for the image-domain listing verb, excluding leading r/w/j and verbs already claimed by RB or JJ (tally, vouch, summon, plumb, scout, parade, etc.):

| Verb | Colophon shape | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **audit** | `rbw-iah/iar/iae` | formal, completeness ceremony fit |
| **count** | `rbw-ich/icr/ice` | basic, plain |
| **chart** | `rbw-ich/icr/ice` | collides with count on first letter |
| **glean** | `rbw-igh/igr/ige` | gather/collect tone |
| **index** | `rbw-iih/iir/iie` | technical-direct; double-i in colophon is awkward |
| **probe** | `rbw-iph/ipr/ipe` | investigative tone |
| **spell** | `rbw-ish/isr/ise` | enumerate-one-by-one origin |
| **field** | `rbw-ifh/ifr/ife` | military "field a team" sense |
| **chant** | `rbw-ich/icr/ice` | collides with chart/count |
| **gauge** | `rbw-igh/igr/ige` | collides with glean; measurement tone |
| **bugle** | `rbw-ibh/ibr/ibe` | military call-to-assembly |

Of these, the ones that both fit the ceremony tone of rekon/wrest/jettison and avoid colophon-letter collisions with each other: **audit, glean, probe, spell, field, bugle**.

My pick: **audit** ŌĆö closest semantic match (complete enumeration with discipline), distinctive lead-letter, reads naturally as "Director audits hallmarks." **Glean** is the runner-up if you want softer ceremony tone.

Which one?

content.content: Updated task #1 status

1	# jjezs_paddock ŌéŻBB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ŌéŻA_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks ŌĆö kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test ŌĆö all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine ŌĆö a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health ŌåÆ marshal-zero ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract ŌĆö load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director ŌĆö all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch ŌĆö they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails ŌåÆ fixture stops ŌåÆ operator stops ŌåÆ debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape ŌĆö partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy ŌĆö is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test ŌĆö cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ŌéŻA_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ŌéŻA_'s remaining paces; ŌéŻA_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse ŌĆö locked design decisions (ŌéóBBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ŌéóBBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ŌéŻBB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ŌéŻA_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks ŌĆö kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test ŌĆö all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine ŌĆö a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health ŌåÆ marshal-zero ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract ŌĆö load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director ŌĆö all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch ŌĆö they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails ŌåÆ fixture stops ŌåÆ operator stops ŌåÆ debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape ŌĆö partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy ŌĆö is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test ŌĆö cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ŌéŻA_

Runs concurrently with ŌéŻA_'s remaining paces; ŌéŻA_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse ŌĆö locked design decisions (ŌéóBBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ŌéóBBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1ŌĆō26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected ŌĆö it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today ŌĆö bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 ŌĆö family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor ŌĆö lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ŌéŻA_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen ŌĆö surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` ŌĆö current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` ŌĆö `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` ŌĆö upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster ŌåÆ **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster ŌåÆ **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM ŌåÆ RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ŌéŻA_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen ŌĆö surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` ŌĆö current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` ŌĆö `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` ŌĆö upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_reslate BBAAN

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (ŌéóBBAAQ spec, ŌéóBBAAR handbook, ŌéóBBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision ŌĆö JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays ŌĆö singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (ŌéóBBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` ŌĆö six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ŌéóBBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent ŌĆö formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision ŌĆö is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast ŌĆö DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **ŌéóBBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **ŌéóBBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **ŌéóBBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **ŌéóBBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (ŌéóBBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (ŌéóBBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (ŌéóBBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
- Image-domain musterŌåÆaudit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

# jjezs_reslate BBAAC

## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy ŌĆö the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** ŌĆö `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** ŌĆö `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** ŌĆö `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** ŌĆö `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Roster + verify** ŌĆö `rbw-arr` (retrievers) and `rbw-adr` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** ŌĆö `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** ŌĆö phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** ŌĆö phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)

# jjezs_reslate BBAAG

## Character

Cleanup phase plus the actual first run. The tier doesn't ship until it's been run end-to-end against fresh state and surprises captured. Failures during the first run are *informative*, not blockers ŌĆö they document the surprises this whole heat exists to surface.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so divest colophons are available and unmake-absorbs-governor-cleanup is in effect.

## Work

### Cleanup phase implementation

Add Phase 5 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Abjure all hallmarks** ŌĆö `rbw-fA` for each ordained hallmark; verify clean removal via `rbw-irh`.
2. **Divest operational SAs** ŌĆö `rbw-arD` for retriever, `rbw-adD` for director (BBAAN cult-verbs). Per BBAAN: divest reads installed RBRA, performs REST DELETE, removes the role's production RBRA file. Verify clean removal via `rbw-arr` and `rbw-adr` roster (empty observation facts expected).
3. **Unmake depot** ŌĆö `rbw-dU` PayorUnmakesDepot. Per BBAAN: governor SA cleanup folds into unmake (404-tolerant on absent governor). No standalone forfeit-governor step. Verify quota recovery.
4. **Final tally** ŌĆö `rbw-dl` shows no depots, `rbw-irh`/`rbw-irr` surfaces clean GAR namespace, `rbw-arr`/`rbw-adr` roster empty.

### First end-to-end run

Execute the FIRST end-to-end run of `rbw-tP` against actual fresh state:

1. Marshal zero, commit (using the new signature from Phase 0)
2. Install RBRAs from secure backup
3. Run `rbw-tP` ŌĆö full sequence; expect ~1 hour wall-clock
4. Capture every surprise ŌĆö failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX ŌĆö in a notch dedicated to this pace's findings
5. Fix small surprises inline within this pace (one-line bash fixes, message clarity)
6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

# jjezs_slate image-muster-to-audit-rename

## Character

Mechanical rename. JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`); RB's image-domain muster verb collides. Per paddock cult-verb naming decision: image muster ŌåÆ **audit**. This pace sweeps all landed image-muster sites in code, tabtargets, and project-memory files.

No design decisions in scope. The rename target and case rule are paddock-locked. Symbol-substitution discipline only.

Depends on BBAAQ landing the spec-side rename first so spec is source-of-truth at code-rename time.

## Scope

**Bash:**

- `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh` (L122-129) ŌĆö `RBZ_MUSTER_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` ŌåÆ `RBZ_AUDIT_*`; `buz_enroll` function refs `rbfl_muster_*` ŌåÆ `rbfl_audit_*`; verb-list comment.
- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` ŌĆö three function defs (`rbfl_muster_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements` ŌåÆ `rbfl_audit_*`); `buc_doc_brief` strings ("Muster hallmarks..." ŌåÆ "Audit hallmarks..."); `buc_success "Muster complete..."` strings; comments at L19-20 (verb listings).
- `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:524` ŌĆö comment ("shared by tally, rekon, muster, batch_vouch" ŌåÆ "audit").

**Rust:**

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs:41-46` ŌĆö three constants `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_MUSTER_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` ŌåÆ `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`; verb-list comment at L41.

**Tabtarget files (rename, content unchanged ŌĆö they're colophon-agnostic launchers):**

- `tt/rbw-imh.DirectorMustersHallmarks.sh` ŌåÆ `tt/rbw-iah.DirectorAuditsHallmarks.sh`
- `tt/rbw-imr.DirectorMustersReliquaries.sh` ŌåÆ `tt/rbw-iar.DirectorAuditsReliquaries.sh`
- `tt/rbw-ime.DirectorMustersEnshrinements.sh` ŌåÆ `tt/rbw-iae.DirectorAuditsEnshrinements.sh`

**Project memory:**

- `CLAUDE.md:84` ŌĆö RBSIM acronym mapping. If BBAAQ renamed the spec file (RBSIM ŌåÆ RBSIA), update both the acronym key and the file path here.
- `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md:103-105` ŌĆö three image table rows (colophon, frontispiece, purpose).
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/CLAUDE.consumer.md:50` ŌĆö verb table entry for "muster".

**Permission file (operator-owned, surface only):**

- `.claude/settings.local.json:1057-1059` ŌĆö three tabtarget shim references. Surface to operator with the proposed three replacements; do not silently rewrite.

## Verification

- `tt/` lists three new `rbw-ia*.DirectorAudits*.sh` tabtargets; old `rbw-im*.DirectorMusters*.sh` files absent.
- `grep -rni 'muster' Tools/rbk/ tt/` returns zero hits in image-domain code paths (SA-domain roster references ŌĆö if BBAAN landed ŌĆö and JJ-internal jjk/ references excluded).
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` builds clean (Rust constant rename compiles).
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` passes ŌĆö fast suite covers manifest validation; rename should not break enrollment-validation.
- `tt/rbw-iah.DirectorAuditsHallmarks.sh` invokable end-to-end against the canonical depot (smoke test).
- `CLAUDE.md` and `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` acronym/colophon mappings consistent with new filenames.

## Constraints

- Spec-side rename (RBSIM content + possible file rename to RBSIA, plus RBS0 image-muster section) lands first via BBAAQ. This pace begins after BBAAQ commits.
- Symbol substitution only ŌĆö no behavioral changes, no test additions, no helper extractions.
- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- BCG compliance for any incidental touch beyond pure rename.

## Out of scope

- SA cult-verb roster work (BBAAN ŌĆö separate domain, separate pace).
- Spec/README/RBSIM file content (BBAAQ ŌĆö already in flight).
- New audit functionality, behavioral changes to listing semantics.
- `.claude/settings.local.json` rewrite beyond surfacing the three references for operator review.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIR-image_rekon.adoc:18` xref to `{rbtgo_image_muster}` ŌĆö owned by BBAAQ's spec sweep.

9f2714bc jjb:1011-9af836de:ŌéŻBB:S: image-muster-to-audit-rename
9af836de jjb:1011-f065916f:ŌéóBBAAT:n: BBAAT depot-list-correctness-sweep against BBAAS landed code (commit 4d810db7). Five mechanical correctness fixes to zrbgp_depot_state_emit and one constant deletion. (1) Drop billingEnabled=true filter ŌĆö DELETE_REQUESTED projects auto-disable billing, so the filter was hiding the very lifecycle the docket wanted observable. (2) Reorder CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list ŌĆö design conversation surfaced three options (a/b/c). Option (b) selected: CRM-first as a lifecycle gate, but still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED so the empirical question 'does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?' stays observable rather than short-circuited away. CRM is the cheaper call and the conceptually-prior question ('is this project alive?'); SA list is the discriminator ('does it have a Mason SA?'). New shape: {Billing list ŌåÆ CRM lookup ŌåÆ lifecycle gate ŌåÆ SA list ŌåÆ Mason scan ŌåÆ emit} replacing {Billing list ŌåÆ billing-enabled filter ŌåÆ SA list ŌåÆ Mason scan ŌåÆ CRM lookup ŌåÆ emit}. (3) Add rbgu_http_code_capture check on CRM lookup ŌĆö replaces 'rbgu_http_json ... || true' followed by jq '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"' fallback (silently lossy on HTTP errors). New path treats non-200 CRM as observability event with explicit log line, then continue. Lifecycle classification stays {ACTIVE ŌåÆ COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED ŌåÆ DELETE_REQUESTED, else ŌåÆ skip-with-log}. (4) Add nextPageToken pagination on Billing v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects and IAM v1 projects/${id}/serviceAccounts. Both wrap the call in a 'while :; do' loop with rbgu_urlencode_capture on the pageToken (BCG-blessed _capture function). Per-page infixes 'depot_state_billing_${page}' and 'depot_state_sa_${global_project_index}_${sa_page}' keep response files distinct. SA-list inner loop breaks on first Mason SA hit (depot's Mason SA is the singleton); on miss, advances page until nextPageToken empty. Global project index threads across billing-list pages so per-project CRM/SA infixes don't collide between pages. (5) Delete orphan RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN constant from rbgc_Constants.sh ŌĆö Mason SA presence IS the discriminator post-BBAAS, BROKEN was never emitted, comment in rbgp_Payor.sh updated to drop the constant reference. BCG audit caught and fixed two violations in the agent's first draft: (a) 'test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break' inside the SA-list loop on Mason hit ŌĆö BCG forbids '&& break/continue/return' for control flow (relies on set -e exemption); fixed to 'test -z "${z_mason_email}" || break' (inverted test, || form). (b) Raw '$(jq -r .nextPageToken // empty <file>)' for nextPageToken extraction ŌĆö BCG forbids $() on external commands (the rbgg_Governor.sh precedent uses raw jq but predates rbgu_json_field_capture's existence); fixed to 'rbgu_json_field_capture <infix> .nextPageToken' which is a _capture function blessed for $(). Empirical check stays open per docket: behavior of IAM v1 serviceAccounts list against DELETE_REQUESTED projects is observable in the new code (DELETE_REQUESTED + Mason SA ŌåÆ moniker emitted; DELETE_REQUESTED + IAM-hides-SAs ŌåÆ 'no Mason SA' log); answers when service-tier verification (BBAAM) resumes. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement preserved (regime-min=0 but required at runtime here). Mason discriminator regex unchanged. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite 24/27 regime-validation matches BBAAS baseline exactly ŌĆö same 3 rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' from blank-template post-marshal-zero state, none exercise rbgp_* code; not regressions from this pace. 47/47 enrollment-validation. BBAAM verification path unblocks: resumes at next service-tier credential window to re-run pristine cases 2/3 against the now-corrected depot_list, with the empirical IAM-on-DELETE_REQUESTED question observable in operator output.

Function-name drift accepted per operator scope ruling: spec now says rbfl_audit_*, bash file rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh still has rbfl_muster_* ŌĆö same shape as cult-verb code-pending; sibling pace renames bash. (4) BUS0 confirmed muster-free pre-edit (zero references, no-op). README confirmed muster-free pre-edit ŌĆö Surface 3 not yet authored will use new vocabulary natively. Out-of-scope per operator's explicit ruling and untouched: bash files (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh rbfl_muster_*, rbgg_*muster* not yet minted in BBAAN), tabtarget files (rbw-im* still on disk), CLAUDE.md acronym map (RBSIM still mapped, frontispiece references DirectorMustersHallmarks etc. unswept), pace dockets in jjg_gallops.json, BBAAN paddock document. CLAUDE.md additive-only discipline preserved ŌĆö only my files (RBS0-SpecTop.adoc, RBSIMŌåÆRBSIA rename) committed; unrelated dirty files in working tree from concurrent BBAAS officium not touched. Two surfaces remain in BBAAQ pending: Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention, will use roster/audit vocabulary natively) and BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

edd19438 jjb:1011-9038b3d7::i: OFFICIUM 260427-1017
9038b3d7 jjb:1011-4d810db7:ŌéóBBAAQ:n: Relocate two collaboration-posture bullets from project CLAUDE.md Collaboration Style section into jjk-claude-context.md for portability ŌĆö if Job Jockey is installed in another project, the posture guidance travels with the kit rather than being lost on the source side. (1) jjk-claude-context.md: inserted two bold-prefixed inline notes after the Concepts block and before "Identities vs Display Names", matching the existing peer-note pattern (Concepts/Identities/Case sensitivity/MCP Tool Usage are all bold-prefixed inline). "**Sincerity over efficiency:**" + body about saying-what-you-notice and discovery-through-conversation. "**Docket posture:**" + body about articulate-what-done-looks-like, point-at-sources, Character line earns-its-keep, rest-resists-filling-in. The first bullet's original "This collaborator values sincerity over efficiency" preamble dropped because the bold heading is the gloss ŌĆö redundant. Substance preserved verbatim otherwise. (2) CLAUDE.md: removed the entire "### Collaboration Style" section header and both bullets. Section ceased to exist (was already only those two bullets). Working Preferences section now flows directly into Heredoc Delimiter Selection without the intervening Collaboration Style header. Reasoning emerged across this BBAAQ session: I made up a verbose continuation prompt with pre-decided voicings and pre-listed constants; user diagnosed the failure as me imposing my outline instead of pointing at the docket; we walked back through CLAUDE.md to nudge against that failure mode (commit 32676ca5 demoted Character from a section to a line in CLAUDE.md). Then this commit relocates that already-edited bullet, plus the sincerity bullet, into the JJK include because the user's framing shifted from "slim CLAUDE.md" to "all Job Jockey ideas need to live in the JJK include for portability" (includes auto-load, so accessibility is unchanged; cohesiveness improves). Tools/jjk/CLAUDE.md (kit-internal hygiene note) cautions against modifying the parent project's CLAUDE.md from a kit-development workflow; not blocking here because this is a project-level placement decision by the project owner crossing the boundary deliberately, not a kit-development edit. Five unrelated dirty files in working tree at notch time (rbgc_Constants.sh, rbgp_Payor.sh, rbgp_cli.sh, RBS0-SpecTop.adoc, RBSDL-depot_list.adoc) appear to be another officium's BBAAS depot-list-decouple-from-rbrr work ŌĆö not mine, not in this commit per CLAUDE.md additive-only discipline.
4d810db7 jjb:1011-66cfc8f9:ŌéóBBAAS:n: Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is no longer emitted ŌĆö Mason SA presence is the discriminator, so a project missing a Mason SA is not classified as a depot at all. State machine collapses to {COMPLETE, DELETE_REQUESTED}; the BROKEN constant remains in rbgc_Constants.sh for backward compatibility but no code path emits it. New constant RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT='depot-project' added next to RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT ŌĆö emit now writes a sidecar fact file '<moniker>.depot-project' carrying the canonical project_id since project_id can no longer be composed from ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${moniker}; rbgp_depot_list render reads both fact files keyed by moniker. RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX is fully removed from the depot_list code path. zrbgp_furnish in rbgp_cli.sh gets differential dispatch via local -r z_command='${1:-}' ŌĆö when the command is rbgp_depot_list, zrbrr_enforce and zrbdc_kindle are skipped (zrbrr_kindle still runs to source rbrr.env into env, which is harmless when the file is absent or fields are blank); levy/unmake/install/governor-mantle keep the strict path. Spec sweep: RBSDL-depot_list.adoc fully rewritten to describe the new Billing v1 ŌåÆ IAM SA list ŌåÆ Mason classifier ŌåÆ CRM lifecycle ŌåÆ dual-fact-file flow, BROKEN state removed, COMPLETE/DELETE_REQUESTED only in the display summary; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbtoe_depot_list_update pattern updated to the same new flow (Billing v1 enumeration, Mason SA classifier, CRM lifecycle lookup, dual fact-file emission), dropping the old prose about CRM filter 'projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE' and the rbw-[name]-[timestamp] pattern validation. RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc audited ŌĆö no listing-semantics prose to update; left untouched. Mason SA discriminator robustness: research (general-purpose agent + WebSearch) confirmed mason- prefix is not used by any GCP-managed service or common IaC pattern; residual false-positive risk only from a customer accidentally naming an unrelated SA mason-X under the same billing account, mitigated by anchoring the moniker portion of the discriminator regex to RBRR's ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape. Constants used everywhere instead of hardcoded literals (caught during BCG review: agent's first pass had 'mason-' literal at three sites; fixed to ${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}- with proper inner-quoting in the # operator per BCG:410-415). BCG review also accepted: if-test (vs case) for differential furnish on a single excluded command; || true on CRM lookup with capture-or-default fallback (less explicit than the SA list path's code-check, but works); regex moniker portion left unbounded (RBRR caps at 26 ŌĆö defensible tightening for a future itch, not blocking). Pre-existing inherited patterns (SC2231/SC2295 on the render glob and parameter expansion) confirmed unchanged from old code via git show HEAD. CLAUDE.md modified by another officium left untouched per additive-only discipline. Listing-semantic shift to flag in operator-facing runbooks: today 'depots under MY CLOUD_PREFIX namespace' ŌåÆ new 'depots billed to MY Payor account'. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite regime-validation fixture: 24/27 pass; 3 failures are rv-positive cases (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) all failing on 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' ŌĆö pre-existing post-marshal-zero environmental state from commit 972e1dc3, none of these tests exercise rbgp_* code, so they cannot be regressions from this pace. Verification of pristine-lifecycle cases 2/3 against the reshaped depot_list (the chicken-and-egg surface that prompted this pace) is service-tier and deferred to ŌéóBBAAM verification cycle resumption ŌĆö that pace was paused waiting for this one to land.
