32676ca5 jjb:1011-28250cb1:ŌéóBBAAQ:n: Demote the docket Character bullet in CLAUDE.md Collaboration Style from a section-shaped prescription to a posture line, biasing future dockets toward spare result-articulation rather than full-briefing prose. Emerged from this BBAAQ session: when asked to draft a continuation prompt for the pace's remaining surfaces, the prompt over-packed (pre-listed constants, pre-decided voicings, enumerated behavioral contracts) instead of pointing at the docket and trusting future-you to read it fresh. Conversation-side diagnosis: dense CLAUDE.md primes dense outputs; the previous Character bullet ("Dockets benefit from a `## Character` section ...") modeled section-shaped thinking by naming Character as a *section*, which subtly primed me to fill in *other* sections too (Scope, Constraints, Out of Scope, References) until dockets read like mini-specs and continuation prompts inherited the same shape. New bullet retains the Character idea (load-bearing for distinguishing "intricate but mechanical" attention from "design conversation requiring judgment" attention) but reframes it as a short *line* not a *section*, and adds an explicit restraint clause ("the rest of the docket should resist filling in"). Edit lives only in /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/CLAUDE.md (the project-level CLAUDE.md, not the parent-directory STOP files). No new bullet added, no new collaboration-style protocol named ŌĆö deliberately resisted naming a "Handoff Protocol" to avoid the symmetry-with-Mount-Protocol urge that would have introduced the very sophistication this edit is correcting against. Net length of the bullet roughly stable. Affiliated to BBAAQ as the pace whose session surfaced the failure mode and whose remaining surfaces (2 and 3) are the proximate beneficiaries when next-session future-me reads the new bullet before authoring. Three unrelated dirty files in working tree at notch time (Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh, rbgp_Payor.sh, rbgp_cli.sh) appear to be another officium's BBAAS depot-list-decouple-from-rbrr work ŌĆö not mine, not in this commit per CLAUDE.md additive-only discipline.
28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:ŌéóBBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in ┬¦"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: ŌĆö concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch ŌĆö second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: ŌĆö implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation ŌĆö out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: ŌĆö instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: ŌåÆ :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file ŌåÆ busff_multi_fact ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_single ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_multi ŌåÆ buf_burx_env ŌåÆ buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) ŌĆö not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

4d810db7 jjb:1011-66cfc8f9:ŌéóBBAAS:n: Reshape rbgp_depot_list so the depot signature is Mason SA presence, not project-name prefix. zrbgp_depot_state_emit now queries Cloud Billing API v1 billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects, lists service accounts per project via IAM v1, classifies a project as a depot iff a service account matching ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com$ exists, derives moniker by stripping the mason- prefix from the SA's local-part, looks up lifecycle via CRM v1 projects/${id}, and emits per-moniker fact files. 28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:ŌéóBBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in ┬¦"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: ŌĆö concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch ŌĆö second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: ŌĆö implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation ŌĆö out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: ŌĆö instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: ŌåÆ :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file ŌåÆ busff_multi_fact ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_single ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_multi ŌåÆ buf_burx_env ŌåÆ buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) ŌĆö not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

28250cb1 jjb:1011-84f9f7d8:ŌéóBBAAQ:n: Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in ┬¦"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: ŌĆö concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch ŌĆö second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: ŌĆö implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation ŌĆö out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: ŌĆö instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: ŌåÆ :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file ŌåÆ busff_multi_fact ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_single ŌåÆ buf_write_fact_multi ŌåÆ buf_burx_env ŌåÆ buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) ŌĆö not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ pending: Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb concepts from BBAAN paddock-settled vocabulary), Surface 3 (README glossary for cult-verb triad and observation-fact convention), and the BBAAN-derived handbook reroute work.

input.description: Check README for new glossary entries

content.content:
101:The [Governor](#Governor) [Invests](#Invest) [Directors](#Director) and [Retrievers](#Retriever) with credentials, [Divests](#Divest) credentials no longer needed, and [Rosters](#Roster) issued credentials.
429:The [Vouch](#Vouch) verifier reconstructs Pre-Authenticated Encoding (PAE), decodes the base64url payload and signature, and verifies via `openssl dgst` against embedded attestor keys ŌĆö a minimal, auditable trust chain.
442:- **Pre-deployment audit** ŌĆö know what you are granting network access to before a [Crucible](#Crucible) is [Charged](#Charge)
457:[Airgap](#Airgap) does not mean "nothing external." It means all external content enters through a single auditable gate ŌĆö the [Enshrine](#Enshrine) ceremony ŌĆö rather than ad-hoc network fetches during build.

toolUseResult.stdout:
101:The [Governor](#Governor) [Invests](#Invest) [Directors](#Director) and [Retrievers](#Retriever) with credentials, [Divests](#Divest) credentials no longer needed, and [Rosters](#Roster) issued credentials.
429:The [Vouch](#Vouch) verifier reconstructs Pre-Authenticated Encoding (PAE), decodes the base64url payload and signature, and verifies via `openssl dgst` against embedded attestor keys ŌĆö a minimal, auditable trust chain.
442:- **Pre-deployment audit** ŌĆö know what you are granting network access to before a [Crucible](#Crucible) is [Charged](#Charge)
457:[Airgap](#Airgap) does not mean "nothing external." It means all external content enters through a single auditable gate ŌĆö the [Enshrine](#Enshrine) ceremony ŌĆö rather than ad-hoc network fetches during build.

content.content:
## Cult-verb naming decision ŌĆö domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** ŌĆö each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster ŌåÆ **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster ŌåÆ **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM ŌåÆ RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

# jjezs_slate spec-dangling-xref-sweep

## Character

Mechanical AsciiDoc xref repair. BBAAQ's quoin retirements (Surfaces 2 and 4 plus naming-collision repair) left dead `{attribute}` references in sibling specs that now render as literal text. This pace flips them to the live attributes. No code, no narrative tracks ŌĆö pure .adoc.

Runs in parallel with any non-spec work. All replacement targets are already settled in RBS0.

## Scope

**SA cult-verb retirements (BBAAQ Surface 2):**
- `{rbtgo_director_knight}` ŌåÆ `{rbtgo_director_invest}`
- `{rbtgo_retriever_charter}` ŌåÆ `{rbtgo_retriever_invest}`
- `{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}` ŌåÆ per call site, `{rbtgo_director_divest}` or `{rbtgo_retriever_divest}` (per-role split, requires reading context)
- `{rbtgo_sa_list}` ŌåÆ per call site, `{rbtgo_director_roster}` or `{rbtgo_retriever_roster}`

**RBRR retirements (BBAAQ Surface 4) ŌĆö 11 sibling specs noted by BBAAQ:** RBSGM, RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL, RBSAJ, RBSIW, RBSDY, RBSDI, RBSTB, RBSRR
- `{rbrr_depot_project_id}` / `{rbrr_gar_repository}` / `{rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}` ŌåÆ flip to literal `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` / `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` prose with derivation pattern, matching BBAAQ Surface 4's RBSDE rewrite precedent.

**Image-muster retirement (already prompted to BBAAQ ŌĆö verify if still pending):**
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIR-image_rekon.adoc:18` ŌĆö `{rbtgo_image_muster}` ŌåÆ `{rbtgo_image_audit}`

**Orphan .adoc files** ŌĆö RBSDK-director_knight.adoc, RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc, RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc, RBSSL-sa_list.adoc no longer included by RBS0. Decide: `git rm` or keep on disk un-rendered. Single decision call at mount time.

## Verification

- `grep -rn '{rbtgo_director_knight\|rbtgo_retriever_charter\|rbtgo_sa_forfeit\|rbtgo_sa_list\|rbtgo_image_muster\|rbrr_depot_project_id\|rbrr_gar_repository\|rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/` returns zero hits.
- AsciiDoc rendering shows no literal `{...}` strings in swept files (spot-check a few).

## Out of scope

- New quoin authoring.
- Behavioral or structural changes to spec content beyond xref flipping.
- Code, tabtargets, project memory.
- Operator handbook tracks (BBAAR).
